content: Hey, I'm back from my sabbatical and I'm still trying to process everything I saw in Eastern Europe. I spent 3 months traveling through Poland, Hungary, and Croatia.
Welcome back! That sounds like an amazing experience. What were some of your favorite moments from the trip?
Definitely the rock climbing I did in Croatia. The scenery was breathtaking. And I also visited some amazing bee farms, which was really interesting for me as a beekeeper.
That's great! Rock climbing can be such a thrill. And beekeeping is a unique hobby. Did you learn any new techniques or get any inspiration for your own beekeeping projects?
Yes, I did. I met a beekeeper in Hungary who showed me some traditional methods that I'm excited to try out. And I also got to perform some stand-up comedy sets in Budapest, which was a lot of fun.
That's terrific! It's great that you were able to combine your hobbies with your travel. Did you have a favorite city or town that you visited?

I think my favorite was Krakow, Poland. The food was amazing and the people were so friendly. I ended up spending 2 weeks there and even got to visit the Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial and Museum on June 15th.

Krakow is a beautiful city. The history and culture are so rich. And visiting the memorial and museum must have been a powerful experience. How did you find the food in Eastern Europe compared to what you're used to in the US?

I loved it. I'm a big fan of pierogies and goulash, and I got to try all sorts of local specialties. I even took a cooking class in Budapest where I learned how to make traditional Hungarian dishes like paprikás csirke.
That sounds delicious. Cooking classes can be such a great way to learn about a culture. Did you have a favorite dish that you tried during your trip?
Definitely the paprikás csirke. I've already made it a few times since I got back. And I'm thinking of entering a cooking competition in my hometown of Chicago next year.
That's great! It's always exciting to share your passions with others. Good luck with the competition, and I hope you get to share your love of cooking with the community.

Thanks, I'm looking forward to it. And I'm also thinking of starting a blog to write about my travel experiences and beekeeping adventures. Maybe I'll even get to write about my next trip, which I'm planning for September 2024.
